title: Description
description: An immutable interpretive record attached to a substrate anchor, per design/descriptions-sketch-01.md §The description record. Fold-invisible; peer to events per architecture-sketch-01 A2.

id.description: Stable identifier, scoped per story. Enables cross-reference and linking.

attached_to.description: Anchor reference. One of: event id, effect-locator, proposition, sjuzhet-entry id, or another description id (D3 allows description-on-description).

kind.description: Starting vocabulary from descriptions-sketch-01 §Kinds; extensible per story with documentation (§Extension rule).

attention.description: Categorical level signaling the kind of scrutiny the description demands, per descriptions-sketch-01 §Attention.

text.description: Free-form UTF-8. The substrate does not parse.

authored_by.description: Human id, LLM identifier (e.g., 'llm:claude-sonnet-4-6'), or 'unknown' for imported content.

τ_a.description: Authored time; monotonic per story; shared sequence with events.

is_question.description: Description-as-question routes to an answer-me queue rather than being treated as an assertion. Orthogonal to kind.

branches.description: Explicit branch scope; must be a subset of the anchor's branches at the description's τ_a. Omitted = inherit anchor (D4).

review_states.description: Tuple of review entries. Omitted = no reviews yet.

promoted_to.description: Reference to an event id, if content was promoted from this description into a fact. Immutable audit link.

status.description: Authorial commitment axis; same as events.

metadata.description: Open dict for tooling-specific extension; substrate does not read.

ReviewEntry.description: One review act on this description, per descriptions-sketch-01 §Review state.

reviewer_id.description: Who reviewed — human id or LLM identifier.

anchor_τ_a.description: τ_a of the anchor at review time; staleness tracking key.

PropPlaceholder.description: Prop shape is under-specified at the design-sketch level. Substrate-sketch-05 §Ontology names Props as 'derived, not stored', while descriptions-sketch-01 §Required fields admits proposition-shaped anchors without defining the shape. A future design sketch (candidate: substrate-prop-literal-sketch-01) pins this down. Until then, PropPlaceholder is a permissive 'any object' marker — an honest flag of the gap, not a quiet Python-import.
